title: Callistephus biennis
intro: Callistephus biennis, commonly known as the Chinese calla or wild calla, is a perennial plant species native to Asia and Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Callistephus biennis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Callistephus biennis is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Callistephus biennis is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the order Asterales. The family Asteraceae is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the tribe Cardueae, which is a group of plants that includes thistles, cardoons, and other related species. Callistephus biennis is native to Eurasia and has been naturalized in North America.

The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
